summary.intent: Add a generic webhook ingress system to Moltis that allows running AI agents in response to GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, and other provider webhook events.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the webhooks feature end-to-end: new `moltis-webhooks` crate, gateway integration, HTTP ingress endpoint, web UI settings page, documentation, test scripts, and PR review fixes. PR #575 created and all review comments resolved.

repo: Settings sidebar nav icons are rendered via CSS `::before` pseudo-elements in `components.css` using `[data-section='X']` attribute selectors — NOT from the `icon` property in the JS sections array
There are two style.css files: `src/assets/style.css` (Tailwind output, gitignored) and `src/assets/css/style.css` (committed copy). The HTML loads the versioned Tailwind output via asset_prefix.
Arc::get_mut silently returns None if the Arc has already been cloned — use OnceLock for late initialization of fields on Arc<GatewayState>
The webhook ingress endpoint needed CORS headers with OPTIONS preflight handling so browser-based tools like Hoppscotch web can reach it
Gon data (server-side JSON to JS) is in `crates/web/src/templates.rs` and is how settings pages get initial data
The cron crate pattern (store trait, sqlite impl, service trait, noop impl, gateway wiring) is the reference architecture for new background-execution features
Website i18n uses `scripts/build-i18n.mjs` for string replacement — adding new strings to English requires running the build script to propagate to all language files
Remote IP for CIDR enforcement must be extracted from X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-Ip headers in axum handlers

code.finding: Crash recovery must drain 'received', 'queued', AND 'processing' deliveries on startup — processing deliveries can be mid-execution when server stops
Body SHA-256 hash as dedup key for generic profile is too aggressive — identical test payloads get deduplicated. Only use dedup when sender provides explicit delivery ID headers
Two separate RwLocks for global + per-webhook check-then-record creates TOCTOU gap — use single Mutex to make check-and-record atomic
String byte slicing like &body[..500] panics on multibyte UTF-8 boundaries — use a truncate_str() helper that walks back to a char boundary
auth_config and source_config contain raw secrets — must call Webhook::redacted() before returning from list/get/create/update service methods
GitLab auth uses 'token' config key but the JS modal's auth config builder was routing gitlab_token into the 'secret' branch instead of 'token'
Arc::get_mut(&mut state) silently returned None because state was already cloned before the webhook fields could be set — switched all three webhook fields to OnceLock

workflow: Use `gh api graphql` to resolve PR review threads programmatically after fixing comments
Test scripts for webhooks need `-k` flag for self-signed TLS certs in local dev
Hoppscotch web app cannot reach localhost due to CORS/mixed-content restrictions — direct users to desktop app instead
Background tasks (Bash with run_in_background) return output via task notification files at /private/tmp paths
PR review bot comments can be stale (reviewing older commit) — verify against current code before acting on them

friction: Arc::get_mut silently failing caused webhook ingress to return 404 on every request — no error logged, very hard to debug
Two style.css files caused confusion about which one is served and why icon CSS classes weren't rendering
Settings sidebar icon system is entirely undocumented — the JS `icon` property is unused, icons come from components.css ::before rules
Hoppscotch web app recommended as testing tool but can't reach localhost due to CORS — had to add CORS headers to ingress endpoint and redirect users to desktop app
Multiple rounds of PR review found issues (TOCTOU, crash recovery, secrets leakage, UTF-8 truncation) that should have been caught in initial implementation
OAuth token expiry blocked the session start requiring user to re-authenticate
open_items: Live-refresh chat UI when background workers append messages to an already-open named session (filed as beads issue moltis-2pu) — affects both webhooks and cron
Translations for webhook feature text in non-English website language files (currently stays in English)
CIDR allowlist enforcement — remote IP extraction is now implemented but actual CIDR matching logic was added as a placeholder
Full provider profiles for Linear, Jira, PagerDuty, Sentry — only GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, and Generic were implemented
Response action tools (post comments, merge PRs, issue refunds) — planned in spec but not implemented in this PR
Per-entity session locking to prevent concurrent processing of the same PR/issue/subscription
